title: Process: The diagram shows the process for recycling plastic bottles. Summarise the infor...
description: The diagram shows the process for recycling plastic bottles. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guide_html:
1. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ mô tả quy trình tái chế chai nhựa thành các sản phẩm mới.

→ Dạng: process (linear, multi-stage)

2. Introduction (paraphrase)

The diagram illustrates how plastic bottles are recycled to produce new products.

3. Overview (ý chính)

  • Quy trình gồm 9 bước, bắt đầu từ thu gom và kết thúc ở sản phẩm mới
  • Có thể chia thành 3 giai đoạn:

→ collection & sorting → processing → manufacturing new products

  • Điểm nổi bật:

→ chai nhựa được biến thành pellets, sau đó tạo thành raw material

4. Body chia logic

BODY 1 – Collection \+ sorting

  • Ban đầu:

→ used plastic bottles are placed in recycling bins

  • Sau đó:

→ được collected by trucks

  • Tại trung tâm tái chế:

→ chai được sorted manually

BODY 2 – Processing (mechanical treatment)

  • Tiếp theo:

→ chai được compressed into blocks

  • Sau đó:

→ được crushed into small pieces

  • Các mảnh này:

→ được washed to remove impurities

BODY 3 – Conversion into new materials \+ products

  • Sau khi làm sạch:

→ nhựa được processed into plastic pellets

  • Các pellets:

→ được heated to form raw material

  • Cuối cùng:

→ nguyên liệu này được used to manufacture new products

(e.g. bottles, T-shirts, bags, pencils)

5. Từ vựng ăn điểm

  • recycling bin → thùng tái chế
  • sorting → phân loại
  • compress → nén
  • crush → nghiền
  • wash → rửa
  • pellet → hạt nhựa
  • raw material → nguyên liệu thô
  • manufacture → sản xuất
  • end products → sản phẩm cuối

The diagram illustrates how plastic bottles are recycled into new products.

Overall, it is a nine-stage process that begins with the collection of used bottles and ends with the production of new items. The procedure involves sorting, compressing, cleaning, and transforming the material into reusable raw plastic.

Initially, discarded plastic bottles are placed in recycling bins and collected by trucks, which transport them to a recycling centre. At the facility, the bottles are sorted to remove unsuitable items. The selected bottles are then compressed into large blocks to facilitate handling and transportation.

Next, these blocks are crushed into small pieces, which are subsequently washed to eliminate impurities. The clean fragments are processed into plastic pellets, which are then heated to form raw plastic material.

In the final stages, this raw material is used to manufacture a variety of end products, such as new bottles, clothing, shopping bags, and stationery items.
